{"society_power": 5.5, "state_power": 7.5, "society_change": 2.0, "state_change": -0.5, "key_event": "Liberal Empire concessions: strike legalization, press/assembly liberalization", "reasoning": "The Second Empire's deliberate liberalization\u2014legalized strikes (1864), the First International, press and public-meeting laws (1868), and surging opposition votes\u2014sharply revived civic and labor organization, while costly foreign ventures (Mexico) and conceded parliamentary/press scope slightly dented an otherwise still-strong authoritarian state."}
